description: A bare-metal RISC-V operating system where the Lua VM acts as the kernel (Nyx). It features a ~100 line C hardware shim and runs everything else in Lua/Teal.
repo_notes: CRITICAL CONTEXT: This is NOT a Linux/POSIX environment. The C files (boot.c, stubs.c, interrupts.c) are low-level shims for RISC-V hardware. The 'Nyx' directory contains the actual kernel logic written in Lua/Teal. Focus documentation on how Lua interacts with hardware via peek32/poke32 MMIO and how the Lua VM is integrated into the S-mode boot process. The build system uses a custom Makefile targeting rv64gc with picolibc.
